column1: # Output must be byte-identical to the legacy PG public.git_describe_as_plevel().
# Add new entries here (and keep them passing) whenever a real-world
# git_describe surfaces that breaks the Perl port.
# Release candidates. The legacy PG function appends `vparts[4]` ("RC1") *and*
# then `lpad(vparts[array_upper], 3, '0')` — which for a plain RC is the same
# RC string again (already 3 chars wide). So `v5.42.0-RC1` ends up with the
# RC tag doubled. We preserve the exact legacy bytes.
# Tagged with N commits ahead, abbreviated SHA
# Edge: very old release-style strings still passing through
# (Edge: shorter inputs like `v5.42` are malformed for the legacy PG
# function — it would return NULL via NULL-propagating lpad. We don't
# emit those, so we don't pin them in the corpus.)
